key_themes: Heavy insider buying by directors and executives after the recent offering
Successful ~$198M capital raise to accelerate Beetaloo Basin drilling and target first gas in 2026
Strong operational results including SS-6H well IP20 flow rate of 11.9 MMcf/d
Bullish analyst upgrades with significantly raised price targets (e.g. $74, $60) and Buy ratings
Positioning in Australian natural gas for energy security and net-zero transition support
summary: Tamboran Resources (NYSE: TBN), focused on natural gas development in Australia's Beetaloo Basin, is generating positive sentiment. Multiple directors and insiders have purchased hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of shares following a dilutive equity offering priced at $35, which raised substantial capital to fund accelerated 2026 drilling and production plans. Operational progress, completed funding, and repeated analyst price target increases to well above the current ~$37-38 level outweigh dilution concerns and support a bullish outlook among investors and analysts.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TBN/)[[2]](https://www.cnn.com/markets/stocks/TBN)[[3]](https://www.marketwatch.com/investing/stock/tbn)
sources: 20+ news articles (Yahoo Finance, TipRanks, BusinessWire, StockTitan, company IR), ~15 relevant recent X posts (primarily on insider buying and funding)
